book_claims.claim: 3-day-old broccoli sprouts contain 10-100x higher glucoraphanin levels than mature plants, highly effective in reducing breast cancer incidence in animals
book_claims.mechanism: Glucoraphanin is converted to sulforaphane, a potent activator of phase 2 detoxification enzymes via Nrf2 pathway

supplementary_research.finding: Broccoli sprouts contain 20-50x the concentration of chemoprotective glucosinolates found in mature broccoli heads, confirming the book's 10-100x claim for glucoraphanin specifically.
Broccoli sprout extracts significantly induced phase 2 detoxification enzymes (NQO1, GST) in human clinical trials, validating the Nrf2 activation mechanism described in the book.
Broccoli sprout beverage enhanced detoxification of airborne pollutants (benzene by 61%, acrolein by 23%) in a randomized trial in Qidong, China. Demonstrates real-world chemoprotective effect.

Sulforaphane from broccoli sprouts is the most potent naturally occurring inducer of Nrf2, with evidence for anti-cancer, neuroprotective, and anti-diabetic effects. Myrosinase-active preparations are far superior to glucoraphanin-only supplements.

culinary_pairings.source: Fat from avocado enhances sulforaphane absorption; mild flavor balances bitterness
Acid brightens sprouts and may help stabilize sulforaphane
Healthy fat carrier for fat-soluble isothiocyanates
Mustard provides exogenous myrosinase to boost sulforaphane conversion when sprouts are cooked
Classic open-sandwich topping; omega-3 complements sprout antioxidants
Kaiware daikon sprouts are traditional; broccoli sprouts serve the same role
Sprouts added to scrambled eggs or omelettes as finishing garnish

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Converted to sulforaphane by myrosinase enzyme. Chewing raw sprouts activates myrosinase. Cooking destroys myrosinase but gut bacteria can partially convert glucoraphanin. Adding mustard restores conversion.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Up to 40 mg from raw sprouts after chewing
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Bioavailability ~80% from raw sprouts with active myrosinase. Drops to ~10-15% from cooked sprouts without exogenous myrosinase. Peak plasma levels at 1-3 hours post-ingestion.
High bioavailability. Complements sulforaphane's antioxidant action.
Fat-soluble; pair with olive oil or avocado for absorption.

synergies.description: Same sulforaphane pathway but 10-100x more concentrated
